person.name: Adeliza du Plessis
person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
As heiress of the honour of Belvoir, Adeliza FitzOsulf de Plessis was instrumental in passing this title and its land down through her family, ironically not through her d'Aubigny children and heirs (who became Earls of Arundel) but through her de Tosny heirs who married into the Bigod family who married into the other d'Aubigny family which later became Earls of Belvoir.

The daughter of Osulf the Freyne du Plessis and sister of Grimault of Plessis, Adeliza married first, William (Guillaume) of Aubigny, of Saint-Martin-d'Aubigné (son of Neel Saint-Saveur, Viscount of Cotentin / Coutances).

Her second marriage was to Robert de Todeni who built the Castle of Belvoir and the St. Mary's Priory (1077) there. By Domesday, de Todeni held 80 manors in 13 counties, most of them in Lincoln and Leicester, and six of which had been held by Oself (his father in law).

Adeliza died before Robert, who died in 1088 and was buried at St. Mary's Priory in Belvoir.

sourceList.title: Adeliza FitzOsulf Du Plessis De Todeni, "Find A Grave Index"

zh.lifeSummary: 當Adeliza du Plessis於1027年出生在法國巴-諾門迪奧內可倫比爾斯時，她的父親Osulf Fil Frane de Plessie Seigneur De Belveir49歲，母親Millicent de Montfichet48歲。她和Guillaume d'Aubigny, seigneur de Saint-Martin-d'Aubigny至少有7個兒子和2個女兒。她去世於1088年在英國英格蘭萊希斯特斯爾貝爾沃易 卡素享年61歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭萊希斯特斯爾波泰斯福德。

nameMeanings.name: du
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Hong, Jian, Jing, Li, Ping, Yan, Yang, Yong, Bin, Chan, Cheng.  Vietnamese Thanh, Quang, Cuong, Dung, Hung, Binh, Minh, Duong, Hao, Hien, Liem, Lien.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 杜, meaning ‘birchleaf pear’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by descendants of Du Kang (杜康), an official noted as a winemaker during the reign of the legendary Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ). (ii) from the placename Du (杜), the name of a city (located in Shaanxi province) granted to Du Bo (杜伯, the Earl of Du), head of the state of Du (杜), who was eventually killed by King Xuan of Zhou (reigned 827–782 BC ). After that, the state of Du (杜) was annexed and the name of the state was adopted as a surname. (iii) adopted as a surname by the Du Gu Hun (獨孤渾) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534 AD ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 都, meaning ‘capital’ in Chinese: (i) from the personal name Zi Du (子都), style name of Gong-Sun Yu, an official in the state of Zheng during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) shortened form of the compound surname Gong Du (公都), which is sometimes traced back to Tian, a prince in the state of Chu who was granted the fief Du (都), or alternatively to Gong Du Zi (公都子), an official in the state of Qi.

variants.name: Doo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Jin Ho, Young, Jong, Ling, Mi Sook, Quong, Yi.
English (Surrey): probably a variant of   Dew  .

variants.name: Duh
meanings: Slovenian and Croatian: nickname from duh ‘spirit, ghost’.

variants.name: Do
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Minh, Thanh, Hien, Long, Dung, Anh, Tuan, Hoang, Hai, Quang, Son.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Chi, Sang, Dong, Chan, Han, Ho, Bai, Chien, Lai, Man.
Vietnamese (Đỗ): from the Chinese surname 杜, see Du 1.
Vietnamese (Đồ): from the Chinese surname 涂 (or 塗), see Tu 1.

variants.name: Dou
meanings: Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 竇, meaning ‘hole’ in ancient Chinese: (i) adopted as a surname by Yue Zhu and Yue Long, two sons of Shao Kang, the sixth king of the Xia dynasty (2070–1600  BC ). Yue Zhu and Yue Long are said to have adopted Dou (竇) (meaning ‘hole’) as a surname because their pregnant grandmother had escaped from a rebellion through a hole in the wall before giving birth to their father, Shao Kang, who later recaptured the throne. (ii) traced back to the Dou (竇) family from the Di ethnic group and some other families from the Xianbei ethnic group in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 豆, denoting an ancient stemmed cup of bowl: (i) traced back to some inhabitants in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) adopted as the surname by the Chi Xiao Dou (赤小豆) and Dou Lu (豆盧) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 鈄, referring to a type of ancient vessel for drinking wine: said to be borne by the descendants of the Duke Kang of Qi (died 379  BC ), who was ousted from his throne in the state of Qi and sent into exile. It was said that he was forced to use the vessel Dou (鈄) to cook food during his exile, and some of his descendants inherited Dou as the surname to memorize this period.

variants.name: Yu
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Ming, Yong, Young, Ping, Chi, Chun, Jae, Li, Sung, Jin, Yang.  Korean Chung, Chang, Byung, Chong, Min, Pyong, Myong, Hyong, Moon, Yiu, Inyoung, Shiu.

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 余 (or rarely written as 餘), meaning ‘I’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the second element of the personal name You Yu (由余), the name of a prime minister in the state of Qin during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from Yu (余), the name of a small state during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iii) said to be borne by descendants of Xia Yu, the first king of the Xia dynasty (2070–1600  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 于 (meaning ‘go’ in ancient Chinese) or 於 (often simplified as 于 in Mainland China, meaning ‘depend on’ in ancient Chinese): (i) simplified form of the Chinese character 邘, the name of an ancient state (located in Qinyang in Henan province), which was granted to Yu Shu (邘叔), the third son of King Wen of Zhou (1152–1056 BC ). After it was annexed by the state of Zheng during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ), the Chinese character 邘 (pronounced Yu) was simplified to (于) and adopted as a surname. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Wan Niu Yu (萬忸于) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534 AD ). (iii) adopted in place of the Chinese compound surname Chun-Yu (淳于), because the first element of the surname, Chun, happened to share the same pronunciation with the personal name of the emperor Li Chun, also known as Emperor Xianzong of Tang (778–820 AD ), which was taboo in ancient China. (iv) said to be traced back to Yu Ze (於則), who said to be the inventor of shoes, an official the legendary Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ). (v) from the second element of the placename Shang Yu (商於) in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ).

en.lifeSummary: When Adeliza du Plessis was born in 1027, in Colombiers, Orne, Lower Normandy, France, her father, Osulf Fil Frane de Plessie Seigneur De Belveir, was 49 and her mother, Millicent de Montfichet, was 48. She had at least 7 sons and 2 daughters with Guillaume d'Aubigny, seigneur de Saint-Martin-d'Aubigny. She died in 1088, in Belvoir Castle, Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 61, and was buried in Bottesford, Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Adeliza du Plessis est née en 1027, à Colombiers, Orne, Basse-Normandie, France, son père, Osulf Fil Frane de Plessie Seigneur De Belveir, avait 49 ans et sa mère, Millicent de Montfichet, avait 48 ans. Elle a eu au moins 7 fils et 2 filles avec Guillaume d'Aubigny, seigneur de Saint-Martin-d'Aubigny. Elle est décédée en 1088, à Belvoir Castle, Leicestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 61 ans, et a été enterrée à Bottesford, Leicestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Adeliza du Plessis 1027 in Colombiers, Orne, Basse-Normandie, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Osulf Fil Frane de Plessie Seigneur De Belveir 49 und ihre Mutter Millicent de Montfichet 48. Sie hatte mit Guillaume d'Aubigny, seigneur de Saint-Martin-d'Aubigny mindestens 7 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1088 im Alter von 61 Jahren in Belvoir Castle, Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Bottesford, Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Adeliza du Plessis è nata il nel 1027 nel Colombiers, Orne, Basse-Normandie, Francia, sua padre, Osulf Fil Frane de Plessie Seigneur De Belveir, aveva 49 anni e sua madre, Millicent de Montfichet, aveva 48 anni. Lei avuto almeno 7 figli e 2 figlie con Guillaume d'Aubigny, seigneur de Saint-Martin-d'Aubigny. Morì nel 1088, nel Belvoir Castle, Leicestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 61 anni, e fu sepolta a Bottesford, Leicestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Adeliza du Plessisが1027年にフランスバセ-ノーマンディオルンコロンビエルズで生まれた時，父Osulf Fil Frane de Plessie Seigneur De Belveirは49歳，母Millicent de Montfichetは48歳であった。彼女はGuillaume d'Aubigny, seigneur de Saint-Martin-d'Aubignyとの間に少なくとも7人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1088年にイギリスイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアベルボイル カストルで61歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアボッテスフォードにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Adeliza du Plessis(이)가 1027년 프랑스 배세-노먼디 른 콜롬비어스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Osulf Fil Frane de Plessie Seigneur De Belveir(은)는 49세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Millicent de Montfichet(은)는 48세였다. 그녀는 Guillaume d'Aubigny, seigneur de Saint-Martin-d'Aubigny(와)과 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1088년 영국 잉글랜드 레이스스터실르 벨보이르 카스틀에서 61세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 레이스스터실르 벗츠퍼드에 묻혔다.

pt.Belvoir Castle, Leicestershire, England: Belvoir Castle, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Adeliza du Plessis nasceu em 1027, em Colombiers, Orne, Basse-Normandie, França, seu pai, Osulf Fil Frane de Plessie Seigneur De Belveir, tinha 49 anos e sua mãe, Millicent de Montfichet, tinha 48 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 7 filhos e 2 filhas com Guillaume d'Aubigny, seigneur de Saint-Martin-d'Aubigny. Ela faleceu em 1088, em Belvoir Castle, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 61 anos, e foi sepultada em Bottesford, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ro.Belvoir Castle, Leicestershire, England: Belvoir Castle, Leicestershire, Anglia, United Kingdom

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Adeliza du Plessis родилась в 1027 г. в местности коломбиирс, орн, бассе-норманди, Франция, её отцу по имени Osulf Fil Frane de Plessie Seigneur De Belveir было 49 лет, а матери, которую звали Millicent de Montfichet, 48. У неё было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 2 дочери от Guillaume d'Aubigny, seigneur de Saint-Martin-d'Aubigny. Она умерла в 1088 г. в местности белвоир кастл, лисестиршир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 61 год и была похоронена в местности боттесфорд, лисестиршир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.Belvoir Castle, Leicestershire, England: Belvoir Castle, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Adeliza du Plessis nació en 1027, en Colombiers, Orne, Basse-Normandie, Francia, su papá, Osulf Fil Frane de Plessie Seigneur De Belveir, tenía 49 años y su mamá, Millicent de Montfichet, tenía 48 años. Tuvo por lo menos 7 hijos y 2 hijas con Guillaume d'Aubigny, seigneur de Saint-Martin-d'Aubigny. Murió en 1088, en Belvoir Castle, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 61 años, y fue sepultada en Bottesford, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
